title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
author: JK Rowling
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets" book by JK Rowling?
Options:
A. One sunny morning about a week after arriving at the Burrow, Harry got word from Hogwarts. He and Ron went down to eat breakfast and saw Mr. and Mrs. Weasley and Ginny already at the kitchen table. As soon as she spotted Harry, Ginny knocked her porridge bowl onto the floor with a loud crash. Ginny had a habit of knocking things over when Harry entered a room. She went under the table to retrieve the bowl and emerged blushing bright red like a sunset. Pretending not to notice, Harry sat and took the toast Mrs. Weasley offered.  
B. Harry received a message from Hogwarts on a bright, sunny morning around a week after he had gotten to the Burrow. He and Ron went downstairs to eat breakfast and found Mr. and Mrs. Weasley and Ginny already sitting at the kitchen table. Right when she noticed Harry, Ginny accidentally knocked over her porridge bowl loudly. Ginny tended to knock things over whenever Harry came into a room. She dove under the table to get the bowl and came back up with her face as red as the setting sun. Acting as though he hadn't seen this, Harry sat down and took the toast Mrs. Weasley gave him.
C. Harry heard from Hogwarts one sunny morning about a week after he had arrived at the Burrow. He and Ron went down to breakfast to find Mr. and Mrs. Weasley and Ginny already sitting at the kitchen table. The moment she saw Harry, Ginny accidentally knocked her porridge bowl to the floor with a loud clatter. Ginny seemed very prone to knocking things over whenever Harry entered a room. She dived under the table to retrieve the bowl and emerged with her face glowing like the setting sun. Pretending he hadn’t noticed this, Harry sat down and took the toast Mrs. Weasley offered him. 
D. On a cheerful, sunny morning around a week after he'd gotten to the Burrow, Harry received a message from Hogwarts. He and Ron went down to the kitchen to eat breakfast and found Mr. and Mrs. Weasley and Ginny already sitting at the table. The instant she caught sight of Harry, Ginny accidentally knocked her porridge bowl to the floor loudly. Ginny tended to knock things over every time Harry came into a room. She dived under the table to get the bowl and came up with her face glowing red like a setting sun. Acting as if he hadn't seen, Harry sat and took the toast Mrs. Weasley gave him.
Answer:

As soon as she spotted Harry, Ginny knocked her porridge bowl onto the floor with a loud crash. Ginny had a habit of knocking things over when Harry entered a room. She went under the table to retrieve the bowl and emerged blushing bright red like a sunset. Pretending not to notice, Harry sat and took the toast Mrs. Weasley offered.
options.B: Harry received a message from Hogwarts on a bright, sunny morning around a week after he had gotten to the Burrow. He and Ron went downstairs to eat breakfast and found Mr. and Mrs. Weasley and Ginny already sitting at the kitchen table. Right when she noticed Harry, Ginny accidentally knocked over her porridge bowl loudly. Ginny tended to knock things over whenever Harry came into a room. She dove under the table to get the bowl and came back up with her face as red as the setting sun. Acting as though he hadn't seen this, Harry sat down and took the toast Mrs. Weasley gave him.
options.C: Harry heard from Hogwarts one sunny morning about a week after he had arrived at the Burrow. He and Ron went down to breakfast to find Mr. and Mrs. Weasley and Ginny already sitting at the kitchen table. The moment she saw Harry, Ginny accidentally knocked her porridge bowl to the floor with a loud clatter. Ginny seemed very prone to knocking things over whenever Harry entered a room. She dived under the table to retrieve the bowl and emerged with her face glowing like the setting sun. Pretending he hadn’t noticed this, Harry sat down and took the toast Mrs. Weasley offered him.
options.D: On a cheerful, sunny morning around a week after he'd gotten to the Burrow, Harry received a message from Hogwarts. He and Ron went down to the kitchen to eat breakfast and found Mr. and Mrs. Weasley and Ginny already sitting at the table.
